volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 5.8.1067c - Section 5.8.1093b)

sections.label: Section 5.8.1067c
sections.translation: Do you step a little that way , Thraso . ( THRASO stands aside .) In the first place , I wish you both implicitly to believe me in this , that whatever I do in this matter , I do it entirely for my own sake ;

sections.label: Section 5.8.1070
sections.original: Verum si idem vobis prodest , vos non facere inscitia est .
sections.translation: but if the same thing is of advantage to yourselves , it would be folly for you not to do it .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1071
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1071b
sections.translation: I ’ m of opinion that the Captain , your rival , should be received among you .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1072
sections.translation: Be received ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1072b
sections.original: Cogita modo . Tu hercle cum illa , Phaedria ,
sections.translation: Only consider . I ’ faith , Phaedria , at the free rate you are living with her , and indeed very freely you are living , you have but little to give ; and it ’ s necessary for Thais to receive a good deal .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1075
sections.original: Ut tuo amori suppeditare possit sine sumtu tuo .
sections.translation: That all this may be supplied for your amour and not at your own expense , there is not an individual better suited or more fitted for your purpose than the Captain . In the first place , he both . has got enough to give , and no one does give more profusely . He is a fool , a dolt , a blockhead ; night and day he snores away ; and you need not fear that the lady will fall in love with him ; you may easily have him discarded whenever you please .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1080
sections.translation: What shall we do ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1080b
sections.original: Praeterea hoc etiam , quod ego vel primum puto :
sections.translation: And this besides , which I deem to be of even greater importance ,— not a single person entertains in better style or more bountifully .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1082
sections.original: Mirum ni illoc homine quoquo pacto opus est .
sections.translation: It ’ s a wonder if this sort of man can not be made use of in some way or other .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1082b
sections.translation: I think so too .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1083
sections.original: Riecte facitis . Unum etiam hoc vos oro , ut me in vestrum gregem
sections.translation: You act properly . One thing I have still to beg of you ,— that you ’ ll receive me into your fraternity ; I ’ ve been rolling that stone [*] for a considerable time past .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1084b
sections.translation: We admit you .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1085
sections.translation: And with all my heart .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1085b
sections.original: At ego pro istoc , Phaedria , et tu , Chaerea ,
sections.translation: Then I , in return for this , Phaedria , and you , Chaerea , make him over to you [*] to be eaten and drunk to the dregs .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1086b
sections.translation: Agreed .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1087
sections.translation: He quite deserves it . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.8.1087b
sections.translation: Thraso , whenever you please , step this way .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1087c
sections.translation: Prithee , how goes it ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1088
sections.original: Quid ? isti te ignorabant : postquam eis mores ostendi tuos ,
sections.translation: How ? Why , these people didn ’ t know you ; after I had discovered to them your qualities , and had praised you as your actions and your virtues deserved ,

sections.label: Section 5.8.1090
sections.translation: I prevailed upon them .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1090b
sections.translation: You have managed well ; I give you my best thanks . Besides , I never was any where but what all were extremely fond of me .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1092
sections.original: Dixine ego in hoc inesse vobis Atticam elegantiam ?
sections.translation: Didn ’ t I tell you that he was a master of the Attic elegance ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1093
sections.translation: He is no other than you mentioned . ( Pointing to his FATHER ’ s house .) Walk this way .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1093b
sections.translation: ( To the AUDIENCE .) Fare you well , and grant us your applause .
